overview: Bhaskar Institute of Technology and Science (BITS), also known as BITS Rayachoty, is a private un-aided engineering college located in Rayachoty, Annamayya District (formerly Kadapa), Andhra Pradesh. Established to provide technical education in the Rayalaseema region, the institute focuses on creating industry-ready professionals through its undergraduate engineering programs.
accreditation: Approved by the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE), New Delhi. Affiliated with Jawaharlal Nehru Technological University, Anantapur (JNTUA).

coursesAndFees.course: B.Tech in Computer Science and Engineering
coursesAndFees.eligibility: 10+2 with 45% aggregate in PCM + AP EAPCET rank

coursesAndFees.course: B.Tech in Electronics and Communication Engineering
coursesAndFees.eligibility: 10+2 with 45% aggregate in PCM + AP EAPCET rank

admissions.admissionProcess: Admission is conducted through the APSCHE web counseling process based on AP EAPCET ranks. 70% of seats are under the Convener Quota (Category A) and 30% are under the Management Quota (Category B).
admissions.importantDates: AP EAPCET 2026 Engineering Exam: May 12 - May 18, 2026; Counselling: June - July 2026.

facilities: Separate Hostels for Boys and Girls
campus.specialNote: The campus is situated on the Rayachoty-Kadapa highway, providing easy accessibility. It is recognized for its disciplined environment and focus on students from rural backgrounds.

contact.address: Ryt - Kpt Highway, Bandapalli, Rayachoty, Annamayya District, Andhra Pradesh - 516269
